preview_text:
The model favors Brazil at 63%, with Scotland's win probability at 12% — roughly a five-to-one underdog. The draw lands at 25%, suggesting a meaningful chance of a stalemate outcome that keeps the contest live.

The spread between Brazil's majority and Scotland's slim path tilts toward a decisive result. A draw at nearly one-quarter probability sits well above a marginal outcome, indicating competitive balance in the second half of the match even if Brazil enters as clear favorites. The three-way split reflects a match neither heavily skewed nor genuinely close across all outcomes.

The model and sportsbooks align closely on the Brazil win (63% vs. 69%) and Scotland upset (12% vs. 12%). The main divergence appears in draw probability: the model assigns 25% while the consensus sits at 19%. This 6-percentage-point gap suggests modest disagreement on whether stalemate or decisive Brazilian victory is likelier. No market consensus is available yet from Polymarket depth, though its Brazil-win estimate (71%) lies above the model.
